title: Whisper
description: OpenAI's Whisper model family for automatic speech recognition and speech translation, mapping audio input to text through an encoder-decoder transformer.
openingSummary: Whisper is OpenAI's Whisper model family for turning speech audio into text. It uses an encoder-decoder transformer to transcribe spoken audio and, when asked, translate speech into another written language.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Whisper is OpenAI's open-weight speech recognition and translation model family released in 2022. Public materials describe a family of encoder-decoder transformer checkpoints sized from tens of millions to about 1.5 billion parameters, trained to map audio into text tokens for transcription and translation tasks.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: Whisper takes audio waveforms as its primary input. Its outputs are text tokens that decode into transcripts, translated text, or other task-specific strings depending on the requested mode. That audio-to-text interface is what connects Whisper to tokenization and encoder-decoder architecture pages elsewhere in this reference.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: At a high level, Whisper follows an encoder-decoder pattern: an audio encoder reads time-frequency features from the input waveform, and a text decoder generates output tokens conditioned on those encoded representations. Cross-attention lets the decoder read encoder states while it emits text one token at a time.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The registry-linked modules below are the main building blocks to understand before comparing Whisper to text-only transformers. Multi-head attention and cross-attention carry audio context into the decoder, BPE tokenization defines the text vocabulary interface, and layer normalization stabilizes the repeated transformer blocks.

training.title: Training
training.body: OpenAI's public Whisper paper frames training around large-scale weak supervision from web-scale speech data, with robustness across accents, background noise, and languages as a central design goal. The linked citations below are the stable primary sources for dataset framing and training objectives.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: Whisper is useful to read as an audio encoder-decoder system rather than as a chat model with a microphone attached. Readers still need tokenization to understand the text side of the interface, and encoder-decoder architecture pages explain how audio features and text generation connect across modalities.
